pattern: Merge many sorted lists into one by always emitting the globally smallest current head, using a heap keyed on node value to pick it in logarithmic time.
hints: Each list is already sorted, so the next node of the merged result is always the smallest among the current heads of all the lists — you only ever compare front nodes.
Maintain a min-heap of the current head of every nonempty list; pop the smallest, append it, and push its successor so the heap always holds at most one node per list.
Tie-break the heap by a unique counter (not the node itself) so equal values never force an object comparison; build the answer behind a dummy head and return dummy.next.

edge_inputs.description: mix of empty and nonempty lists
three lists with cross-list duplicate values
disjoint ranges so one whole list precedes another
many single-node lists, descending order across lists

stress.description: many sorted lists whose total length stays within the global cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(0, 50)
    total_cap = max(0, min(size * 5, 10000))
    remaining = total_cap
    lists = []
    for _ in range(k):
        if remaining <= 0:
            lists.append([])
            continue
        m = rng.randint(0, min(remaining, 500))
        remaining -= m
        lists.append(sorted(rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(m)))
    return [lists]
stress.note: each list sorted ascending; sum of lengths bounded
